name: Vista Chino
description:
'''Vista Chino''' was an American [[Rock music|rock]] band formed in 2010 by vocalist [[John Garcia (singer)|John Garcia]], drummer [[Brant Bjork]] and bassist [[Nick Oliveri]], all previously members of [[Kyuss]], along with guitarist [[Bruno Fevery]].

The band formed under the name '''Kyuss Lives!''' as a short-lived venture touring the music of the original Kyuss, with a lineup excluding [[Josh Homme]]. In 2012, the band announced plans to continue touring indefinitely and begin work on a studio album using the Kyuss Lives! moniker. However, a lawsuit by Homme and former Kyuss bassist [[Scott Reeder (bassist)|Scott Reeder]] prohibited the use of the name for recordings, prompting the band to rename themselves Vista Chino. After a tour and one album, ''[[Peace (Vista Chino album)|Peace]]'', Vista Chino broke up in 2014 when Garcia and Bjork supported solo releases.
